name: Texas Jewelry & Loan
description_short: Family-owned pawn shop in Plano, TX since 1980, offering collateral-based loans, jewelry sales, and gun transfers with 45 years of local service.
description_long:
Texas Jewelry & Loan is a family-owned pawn shop established in 1980 by Donna and Ben Bridgefarmer, now operated by their son Steve Bridgefarmer. The business has served the Plano and Dallas-Fort Worth metroplex for 45 years, establishing itself as a longstanding fixture in the local community with deep roots and generational continuity in ownership and operations.

The shop operates as a traditional pawn operation, offering collateral-based loans where customers pledge items (jewelry, guns, and other valuables) to receive cash advances. They also maintain an online shop for retail sales and facilitate gun transfers as a licensed dealer. The company positions itself around customer service excellence and knowledge across multiple product categories, from jewelry appraisals to firearms expertise.

The business distinguishes itself through extended staff tenure and specialization—Chris Fodor has worked there over 20 years and handles repairs and custom window artwork, while Steve brings 24 years of experience and specific expertise in firearms and hunting. The company emphasizes personal relationships and accessibility, encouraging customers to ask for specific staff members and highlighting enthusiasm as a core operational value.

As a pawn shop, this business functions in the collateral lending space where customers exchange possessions for immediate cash—a legitimate but higher-cost financial tool. The website provides minimal detail on loan terms, interest rates, or fee structures, which is typical for pawn operations but limits transparency for prospective borrowers. This remains best suited for customers with items of value who need rapid, short-term cash without credit checks.
pros: 45-year operating history in same market (Plano/DFW) demonstrates stability and community trust
No credit check required for pawn loans—accessible to those with poor or no credit history
Licensed gun dealer offering firearms transfers, a specialized service not all pawn shops provide
Online shopping option available for retail customers, expanding beyond in-store visits
Long-tenured staff (20+ years for Chris Fodor, 24 years for Steve) ensures knowledge and service consistency
Family-owned operation with transparent ownership and accessible contact information
Immediate cash availability—pawn loans offer same-day funding without underwriting delays
cons: Website provides no loan terms, APR rates, or fee structure—critical pricing information is absent
No details on redemption periods, storage fees, or default consequences for pledged items
Limited online functionality; appears to be primarily brick-and-mortar with minimal e-commerce details
Pawn loans carry collateral loss risk—customers forfeit items if unable to repay within agreed timeframe
Website notes uncertain hours as of January 2025 ('hoping roads will allow us to get to the shop'), suggesting potential operational disruptions
best_for: Customers with items of value (jewelry, guns, electronics) needing immediate short-term cash without credit checks
Those with poor credit or no credit history unable to qualify for traditional personal loans
Firearm owners needing licensed gun transfer services in the DFW area
diagnosis: Texas Jewelry & Loan is best for customers with valuable items who need rapid cash and cannot or do not want to pursue credit-based lending. The primary caveat is the complete absence of transparent loan terms—interest rates, fees, redemption periods, and default consequences are not disclosed on the website, making it impossible to assess true cost before visiting or calling.
services: Pawn loans against jewelry, firearms, and other collateral
